authority_note: The initial source inspection used PR #36 head c20adaede64d45c24d74b8531507935ad7a8ce02. Its tree is identical to integrated merge commit 712599ff31599e1a259157dd0e34915d0777d5f6; the final branch was recreated from the integrated merge commit before review.
goal: Introduce one typed employee UI port, high-level web adapters for owner_web, public_form and boundless_website, and swappable theme/layout/component/density strategies that can make Web clients materially different without changing the loaded employee payload.

terminology.port: The stable purposeful employee UI conversation contract.
terminology.adapter: A high-level Web client that implements that port for owner operation, a public bounded form workflow, or an employee-dominant website.
terminology.strategy: Theme, layout, component set, density or brand-token selection inside an adapter.
terminology.ui_lab: A fixture-backed test driver that mounts adapter and strategy combinations; not a product adapter.

non_goals: Do not deploy a public employee homepage.
Do not add a database migration.
Do not replace the current operating-state model or data-loading controller.
Do not create one adapter per theme or layout.
Do not add Storybook, a visual framework or a heavyweight optimization dependency.
Do not claim browser screenshots or production evidence when execution is unavailable.
authority: current shared, Manager, Web, UI Lab, onboarding, profile and browser-test source
research_conclusions: Cockburn's original pattern supports a small number of purpose-defined ports and multiple adapters, not adapters for every visual variant.
Cockburn's Component + Strategy framing is the closer match for radically different themes, layouts and component sets inside one owner Web adapter.
React Context is sufficient to distribute one resolved presentation profile through an existing component tree.
CSS custom properties are the lowest-cost mechanism for ordinary branding and theme changes; extreme variants may later select distinct React component registries.
Next dynamic imports should be introduced only when separate adapter component trees materially increase bundle size.
Playwright projects, screenshots and ARIA snapshots fit the adapter/strategy/viewport test matrix already represented by UI Lab.
observations: The repository already has one operating projection compiler, one layout planner, one renderer registry and one production AgentSurface.
Employee profile key/version, business kind, dominant domains and profile/onboarding facts already reach the Web payload.
Onboarding branding facts can seed scoped design tokens without a schema migration.
Talk and Workspace styling is largely component-local and hard-coded, so the first implementation uses a scoped wrapper and CSS variables rather than a renderer rewrite.
The existing free estimator is the first concrete public_form adapter candidate.
UI Lab already routes fixture payloads through production components and can become the adapter/strategy matrix.
implemented_candidate: optional ui_presentation hints in onboarding and profile package contracts
unknowns: Visual quality and CSS collisions until Playwright runs in a dependency-complete environment.
Which boundless website component tree will become the first productized public adapter.
Where user-selected presentation preferences should be persisted after the UI control is designed.
